name: Factor(UT): Untrusted Decomposer
description: AAAI 2026 workshop paper. Can you safely use a powerful but untrusted AI to plan tasks, while smaller trusted AIs execute the work? Yes — but only if you monitor the execution, not just the plan. Implementation-level monitoring achieves AUROC 0.96 vs. 0.52 for plan-only monitoring.

name: DeceptionScoop
description: When an AI is about to deceive you, does it show physical signs — like a person who pauses before lying? DeceptionScoop tests this: measuring GPU power spikes, response latency, and token entropy shifts during LLM inference to detect deceptive intent before it acts.

name: Levi
description: A personal AI operating system. Persistent memory across sessions, multi-channel presence (Discord, Telegram, Signal), ontology-driven context engine, and autonomous background operations. Levi is the user-facing interface layer of a broader intelligent systems stack — purpose-built to know who you are, what you're working on, and what needs to happen next.

name: Talos
description: Enterprise intelligence platform for capital-intensive industries. Ontology-driven digital twin engine for proposal automation, asset tracking, and supply chain forecasting. Talos connects instrumentation data with structured AI reasoning — designed for industries where decisions are expensive and information is fragmented across systems, teams, and time.

name: Flux
description: Financial intelligence OS. Tracks capital flow, smart money movement, and market structure across DeFi and traditional markets. Flux is built to understand where money actually moves before narratives form — mapping liquidity patterns, on-chain flows, and structural positioning. Not a trading bot. A financial lens for people who think in systems.

name: Node Zero
description: Foundational operational intelligence layer — ontology-driven infrastructure for context management, agent orchestration, and decision support across systems. Node Zero is the substrate beneath Talos, Levi, and Flux: the layer that holds structure, propagates context, and coordinates autonomous components across the full stack.

name: POP2 Protein Expression
description: POP2 is a human enzyme involved in RNA processing. We used AI throughout the workflow to help bridge technical gaps: supporting literature review, lab calculations, protocol refinement, parameter selection, experimental design, and troubleshooting. The result was 735 μg/mL expression yield in yeast and E. coli, with a 10-person undergraduate team operating at graduate-level throughput. CURC Highest Honors.

name: CryoProtect
description: Computational R&D pipeline for cryoprotectant discovery. Curated molecular database of 9,400+ compounds with RDKit-powered similarity search, cheminformatics scoring, and property filtering. Built for a Colorado biotech lab. Archived R&D with future potential — core dataset and codebase remain private.

name: Control Technologies Ltd
description: Service engineering internship in Trinidad. Designed P&ID diagrams for automated brewery control in AutoCAD, implemented CCTV-PLC monitoring for regulatory compliance, and supported industrial site work tied to process and infrastructure operations.

name: LAS Retail
description: Founded the first specialty retail smoke shop in Trinidad by studying U.S. 2018 Farm Bill regulations and mapping them onto local legal gray zones. Ran operations solo: supplier sourcing, compliance, inventory, customer acquisition. Achieved traction, executed a clean exit, and relocated to pursue research.

name: Ontology (gitlawb)
description: Public substrate for foundational ontological primitives. Four-layer architecture: base layer (provenance, identity, assertion, signature), structural layer (relation, taxonomy, context, delegation), real-world benchmarks (66% token efficiency, 94-97% cache hit rates), and patterns for safe autonomous systems. Enables agents and humans to name things durably and reason together with semantics that outlast implementations.
